title: Implement optimistic loading across the app (React Query cache-first mutations + rollback)

requirements.text: Implement consistent optimistic UI updates (optimistic loading) for all key mutations by moving optimistic behavior into React Query mutation hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` using `onMutate` (optimistic cache update), `onError` (rollback), and minimal targeted invalidation/refetch only when required for eventual consistency.

quotes: Kannst du optimistic loading für die ganze app einbauen , das alles schneller lädt

acceptanceCriteria: For each of these actions—post like/unlike, comment add, comment like/unlike, follow/unfollow, post create/delete, profile save—the UI updates immediately without waiting for the backend response.
If a mutation fails, the UI state is reverted to the previous value (no permanent ‘stuck’ optimistic state).
Mutations do not broadly invalidate unrelated queries; invalidation is targeted (e.g., only the affected query keys) and only used when an optimistic cache update is insufficient.
The app still reaches eventual consistency with backend data after mutations complete.

requirements.text: Update UI components to rely on the unified optimistic React Query cache state rather than per-component local optimistic state where applicable, ensuring identical optimistic behavior in all entry points (e.g., liking from feed card vs. post detail modal; liking comments from comments modal vs. post detail modal).

quotes: optimistic loading für die ganze app

acceptanceCriteria: Liking a post from `PostCard` and from `PostDetailModal` shows the same immediate like/like-count changes and the same rollback behavior on error.
Comment like/unlike shows immediate count/icon updates in both `CommentsModal` and `PostDetailModal` comment lists, with rollback on error.
Adding a comment shows the new comment immediately in the list (optimistically) and removes it or marks it failed if the backend request fails.
Follow/unfollow updates follower/following state immediately on profile pages and reverts on error.

requirements.text: Make post create/delete feel instant by applying optimistic cache updates to the feed (`['feed']`) and user posts (`['userPosts', principal]`) lists so navigation back to feed/profile does not require a full refetch to reflect the change.
acceptanceCriteria: After creating a post, the new post appears immediately in the feed and in the author’s profile posts list (optimistically).
After deleting a post, the post disappears immediately from the feed and from the author’s profile posts list (optimistically).
If create/delete fails, the feed/profile lists revert to the prior state.
No full-page loading spinners are required to see the updated feed/profile after these actions; the perceived latency is minimized.

constraints: Do not modify files under `frontend/immutablePaths` (e.g., `frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts(x)`, `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts`, `frontend/src/main.tsx`, and anything in `frontend/src/components/ui`). Compose or wrap instead.
Keep backend as a single Motoko actor in `backend/main.mo`; do not introduce additional backend services.
nonGoals: Adding real-time updates (WebSockets/push) to propagate likes/comments instantly across devices.
Changing backend data models or adding new backend endpoints unless strictly necessary for optimistic UI correctness.
Redesigning the visual theme or layout; this request is performance/UX responsiveness focused.
